All right, I am jumping your page 45 start talking with number 25 Declare war on an establishment or an orthodoxy Again by the way if you did not see Trump's Tuesday night teleprompter speech I Suggest finding it Because it was brilliantly crafted it's really worth seeing He does an entire thing on Much more elegantly than I'm going to do about how the whole government system is rigged to protect the people in it and Therefore you can't expect to send anybody who rigged it to go fix it Therefore this election by the way heads exploded again in Washington This election is not about Republicans and Democrats at all forget political parties This is about us and them Always great. I mean it was just great because it is this It is and it is him against okay you establishment jerks want to push me around. I'll fix you I'll give a speech and tell everybody none of you matter How about that us and them brilliant? Brilliant So this has been which probably loops back by the way to what I said to you earlier. So this has been a big modus operandi of mine for a fairly long period of time is Throw grenades at an establishment of something Because it's a fairly safe bet in almost every environment That there is a significant percentage of people who are there because there's really no place else to go but They hate the establishment that is running the there or they have already concluded that the establishment that is running The there is a bunch of idiots but It's the only place of its kind to go So I mentioned earlier national speakers association so starting in 1978 After I quickly determined the whole thing was run by idiots I Had actually 1979 because 1978 I joined with some optimism and So I had made about a hundred and about 108 grand that year Doing it as primitive as I mean dumb stupid Literally door knocking and cold calling get speaking game. I mean you can't be dumber, but I've made over a hundred K And so I went to the NSA winter workshop Optimistic that Jesus if I managed to grind out a hundred K stupid, right if I get smart Imagine right and so by noon the first day I've I'm pretty depressed Oh Because what I discover is 70% of the people are better at the technical aspect of the thing than I am so they're all better speakers at this point I mean because I suck so they're all better But none of them not to make any money and in the earliest conversations. I made the mistake of hey I'm doing I know I'm doing this stupid Right and I managed to grind out about a hundred and five grand this year so and the conversation stopped there with you did what How did you grind out a hundred and five grand I'm going well, this is not the conversation I was planning on having This is not this is not exactly how I thought this was good to go so I Took a lot of money out of NSA from members for a number of years by attacking and I say Didn't win me any friends at NSA by the way A lot of them are old so most of them have died, but there's a few who are still you know pissed off Well, there are there's people that are genuinely pissed off I Mean some of them carried a grudge for 30 years But I've had that First job I had my only job I ever had this national sales manager of that company 20 years after I quit I saw him at the book expo when he was still pissed at me so I'm still pissed off at you I didn't barely remembered you. What are you doing thinking about me? I'm not thinking about you You know when I left I was mad at you too, but you know 20 years John gets okay You son of a bitch you So here's what I immediately found as soon as I started to say you know this whole NSA thing they're they're wrapped to you guys of Grovel for 10 years and then maybe you can start to make some money and for God's sake don't sell anything unless you beg for permission and You know start charging low fees and you need to work with bureaus so you have to be bureau friendly Which means you have to kiss the ass of the five guys that own bureaus. That's what that means This really doesn't make sense guys this is stupid and There's a better way well there was already a bunch of people in there who were there only because it's the only there But they were already thinking to themselves No, this doesn't make sense to me It's the way everybody says to do it, but it doesn't make sense to me and for the most part they were the smartest ones there They were business guys who were going on that makes sense to me They were just waiting for somebody to face them and say that And so the more I crapped on the establishment and I say the more money I made From the relatively small but still significant percentage who were really already there They were just waiting loop back all the way to this morning for somebody to validate What they were already thinking? You know what this doesn't make any damn sense And boom they come Chiropractic When we had our own companies in chiropractic. That's the establishment of Advertising that's what it is 80% of all advertising is image and brand advertising only 20% is direct response It is incidentally the 80-20 rule Perfectly applied the 20% is making more money than the 80 but but that's neither here to there So the establishment of advertising is traditional brand and image advertising From book one article one speech one I crapped all over and That works so In almost every place there's some orthodoxy That you can pick a fight with and That you can position yourself Against For sales people for me It has always been Sales managers The suits at the home office who do sales people hate They hate their sales managers. and you want to use it When you pick enemies and villains your page 46 This is a really useful take home So there's seven items enunciated there about Using enemies and villains for your benefit So one is that no movement ever rises without belief in a devil So you need a devil Secondly Secondly The excitement The movement is proportionate to the tangibility and vividness of the devil So if they're vague and ambiguous That's one thing So everybody knows for example say all salespeople know sales managers are idiots but when I Do a longer version of what I just did and personify it and describe Chalk the sales manager who I worked for that's more vivid And then everybody can identify because they have one three It's essential to have a tangible enemy Not an abstract one They got to be able to see it touch it statistically understand it I'll skip over four five this is important The ideal devil is one So if there's more you got to figure out how to tie them together and concentrate them together has one big devil That everybody can focus on Six and this is cool when you do this So anything you do that doesn't work The way it's supposed to that is visible in front of the people you have paying attention to you It's not your fault It's the fault of the devil who won that battle Which makes it all important all the more important for you guys to band together against this evil So you now have a get out of jail free card for anything you do That doesn't work if you've done this well And it is important that The the devil ideally is an invader is a foreigner from outside Not from inside So here's a kia did the graphics this is not a new idea but So Walmart For retailers Has been for at least 10 years a pretty reliable devil And they qualify on almost Uh, the free report offer is the end of the independent pharmacy Uh, there are very reliable evil that can be beat up A lot and retailers are right there because they blame all of their Lack of success on this devil. They're just waiting to have you validated Now first of all, it's factually untrue In that if you take walmart, for example every time walmart opens a new store two things happen Some area retailers die Other ones do better than they ever did before Because walmart creates a lot of traffic And walmart stocks only stuff that large numbers of people will buy They don't stock stuff that only small numbers of people will buy So they create a host parasite relationship for the ones that are smart enough to be in orbit around them But change the merchandise mix So an independent hardware store, for example, can do real well even though there's a walmart and a home depot in the same General vicinity if he knows what he's doing. So it isn't even true It's only partially true Right, they still have to volunteer to be victimized By their own behavior When walmart comes in now if they bend over walmart will give it to them But that that's theirs really it's not walmarts, right In healthcare We This is one of the few reasons i'm going to say this But because I have clients selling to healthcare Professionals we will be sorry to see obama go Because as the devil behind obama care He's been great I mean it's like gold Right you wave him and obama care and pretty much anybody in any healthcare business Their hackles rise and they're aggravated and they're pissed off and they're scared and they're frightened And oh my god, what is going to happen next and that's exactly where we want them to be He's also been really good as you probably know for the gun business um, I mean great Every time he's mouss off about something That has to do with guns everybody runs out and buys a bunch of guns and ammo He's like I mean gun shops actually have his picture up with a thing underneath america's number one gun salesman I mean i'm serious. They actually do right The donnelly is it unintended consequences? I assume it's actually reverse consequences of what it is that he'd like to have happen, but so these And of course you see trump doing this very effectively Creating a villain his villains match the criteria i gave you here perfectly I mean perfectly so whether it's instinctive or whether he got it out of the book I don't know but He does it very very well so Most people think so here's what all Bad marketers bad salespeople bad advertisers And bad copywriters always do Is they default to? product or service feature or benefit price and offer and some claim of comparative superiority So I sell x whatever x is x has Glow in the dark feet And that's a benefit because you won't fall down the steps and x is nine dollars, but If you order now you get a second one free just pay separate shipping and processing And by the way, it's better than the other guys x because it's made of palladium That's what everybody 90 of all advertising marketing and selling is this if you Pick anything pick a magazine watch tv for a night I mean That's what most of it is and That's like the least important thing we could ever do All this other stuff will move a lot more merchandise right and so when you Create an evil Or latch on to an evil they already know is there and you make On this you're on their side in opposition to this Dangerous and frightening nasty villain Who is coming to take them away? In some way shape or form and now here's how embedded this is So most of what we're talking about today is Going all the way to the point of triggering something that is deeply embedded in people's Psyche's I've mentioned it a number of times just understand. It's all about that So here's what a hundred percent Of people have in common. There's not a lot that a hundred percent of people have in common Here's something all hundred percent of the people have in common has children There were boogie men under the bed Or in the closet there was somebody coming to get them And hurt them or spirit them away and they were scared of them And usually a parent fixed it That's deep in there And so it's just waiting to be Hit with a sharp pointed stick and there it is again So when you create a boogie men under the bed That you can be the crusading person on a what you can be the dad or mom who comes in Looks under the bed Make sure they're not there Or lies to you and says I got them You have to worry about it anymore I got them right here and i'm dragging his little green slimy ass out of here Some parents do it that way some parents do it the other way But most parents do one or the other when you are that you gain enormous advantage And it has nothing to do with whether the hose is made out of palladium or not I mean we got to get there eventually, but the key word is eventually Let's jump to 29 Which is the bigger idea So david ogle v the great ad guy One of his questions that he said he would often ask and get unsatisfactory answers to When people came to him with ads they had put together in the agency was okay fine, but what's the big The big idea of this And when there's a big driving idea it gets easier to sell stuff and to influence people so In the probably started post-world war two 1950s In homes for relatively middle class and up You may have had one in a home when you grew up They began to put in music rooms And there was a room for music This was driven by a huge amount of money spent For three years before it really became popular By the piano industry So the piano industry had a concentrated Lobbying marketing Handing out goodies effort to architects builders media Everybody that would affect a home buyer Lobbying for the idea that there should be a music room If you have a music room, what do you need? A piano Right now we don't have to sell pianos People got to go buy pianos and they do More recently What did all new big homes start to be built with? Theaters of course got to have a theater Another big idea recently. It's called the man cave You got to have a man cave If you buy that big idea You got to have a man cave You can't just have a cave We're not the Flintstones I mean even they had some rock furniture You got to stock this thing with something It's the big idea Home office People have a home office that don't do nothing But you got to have a home office All right, so this big idea that becomes the driving force for all sorts of purchasing activity Is another thing to try and think through In your own mind, how can I create a big idea? That automatically drives interest in What it is that I sell So as you know I love fax machines It's one of the few pieces of technology I like By the way, there is an entire book called fax Which is the entire history of the fax machine I suspect I'm the only person who bought a copy And why anyone would publish this is beyond me But it is 300 solid pages of everything you ever wanted to know about a fax machine So this guy was even more fascinated with them Than I am but many of you will not remember there was a time when nobody had fax machines And they had they had to get fax actually try to service called ZAP Where they had fax machines in the trucks And you could Pay them to fax for you because the Problem of course is you had to fax to FedEx at the other end And they had to deliver it because nobody had fax machines So the service was sort of ill conceived because as soon as everybody got fax machines the business was over but so The big idea was instant Hey, you can take this document And you could have it appear at the other end exactly like you got it here like this How many documents do you really have to have that happen with? Not a lot I mean Next day was really sort of sufficient For most people's purposes But Instant became the big idea All right, I wanted to be instant And of course now as you know, there's a whole plethora of things that Arguably make this unnecessary Way back and still today to a great extent so Millions of new businesses get started every year What is the one thing every new business owner does And pretty much does has one of the first few things every new business owner does Business card. Yeah, you got to have a business card. Why do you got to have a business card? Who says you got to have a business card? The whole idea of business cards had to be popularized and it became a big idea and then it became Kind of mandatory if you don't have a business card, you're not like in business By the way, how many of you got business cards? So you just gave me the brand new kind of It's a business card. So after all these years, I now have a business card again Okay But so so business cards are so Uniquitabous has a big idea that vista print is able to advertise them on television television Because it's everybody is accepted You've got to have one It's the thing to do when you get business. This is what you do For years they've tried to make incorporation in LLCs the same And they get closer and closer and closer, but still it ain't to this point So this is a big idea what you gotta have now what they have managed to do it with is a website Most people believe you got to have one And so if they don't do anything else they go to one of the cheap services like one to one that you see them advertised on tv and you can Drop and load a picture and there's eight templates and they're by the way for a lot of people's purposes these things are fine But they we've managed to get people to believe you've got to have a website You guys all think you've got to have a smartphone So it's only a question of brand and model Everybody's going to have one the rolls out there by the way these rolls in 1986. Yeah, I don't have it activated, but you could activate it So we accept these things that oh, okay, this is now a norm This is what everybody around here does This is what we've got to have And so everybody does it if you can figure out how to make that happen Party plan selling in the 1960s The Tupperwares of the world This was an accepted Suburbia activity And you had to go So ask your moms some of you a few of you got to ask your grandma
